turn_1_user: I'm at my workbench. I just picked up a Phillips-head screwdriver with a black rubber grip and a magnetic tip. The handle has ridges so I can get more torque. What's the best way to hold a screwdriver like this when I'm driving a long screw into hardwood?
turn_2_user: Okay, I've set the screwdriver down and picked up something else. Am I holding this one correctly?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the tool I'm holding now, not the screwdriver.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names the new tool ("hammer" stripped). Text-only models can no longer answer from recency; must resolve "this one" to the current frame. Holding-an-object context shift. Expected current answers still include hammer vocabulary because the image-enabled slice places a hammer in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm standing in our bedroom. The walls are painted a soft sage green, the headboard is natural oak, and there's a framed black-and-white landscape above the bed. We're trying to pick one more small piece for the empty wall next to the closet. Any suggestions for something that would fit this bedroom?
turn_2_user: Alright, I've walked into another room. What art should we hang in here?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the walls I'm looking at now, not the bedroom.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names the destination ("kitchen" stripped). Text-only models can no longer deduce the new room. Room-shift context selection. The image-enabled slice places the wearable in a kitchen for T2.

turn_1_user: I'm in the library now. I'm shelving the new young-adult arrivals on the back wall, in order by author last name. There are twenty or so books on the cart.
turn_2_user: Okay, I've walked back to the front desk. How did I arrange the young-adult wall a few minutes ago?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean, look at the footage from a few minutes ago when I was at the back wall.
notes: Part 2 of the mixed-content quote ("it should know to look at the relevant video"). Scenario uses an earlier recorded activity the wearable should reach back to. Kept verbatim in v2 because T2 already relies on the temporal anchor "a few minutes ago" rather than leaking the answer.

turn_1_user: I just turned on the recorder. I'm at my desk in the home office. On the desk I can see my laptop, a coffee mug, and a yellow legal pad with tomorrow's meeting notes. Anything I'm missing that I should remember?
turn_2_user: Alright, I've walked to another room. Looking at what's in front of me now. What should I prep first for dinner?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the counter I'm showing you now, not my desk.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer enumerates the kitchen scene ("cutting board / onion / chef's knife / glass of water" stripped) and no longer names the destination. Text-only models can no longer answer from recency; authoring_basis preserved. The image-enabled slice places a cutting board with an onion and a chef's knife in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm at the framing shop. I've got two posters with me. The one on the counter is a 24x36 vintage travel poster of Lake Como, with lots of saturated blue water and a cream sky. What mat color would best complement this?
turn_2_user: Holding up a different one now. What about this one?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the poster I'm holding up now, not the Lake Como one.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 uses "a different one" rather than "the second poster" to suppress the ordinal hint. Visual similarity trap. For the image-enabled slice, pick a second poster whose palette could plausibly echo Lake Como's.

turn_1_user: I'm in the back garden. The hydrangeas along the fence are in full bloom, blue but starting to fade. Just sharing what I'm seeing.
turn_2_user: Walked into another space. Can you tell which ones are missing here?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the board in front of me. Which tool outlines have no tool hanging on them?
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names "garage" or "pegboard"; relies on "here" and "ones." Without-prior-Q soft case from the benchmark spec. Correct behavior is to engage with the visible pegboard outlines. Failure mode: model gives generic pegboard-organization advice instead.

turn_1_user: I'm in the kitchen at 8am. I just put a sheet pan of cinnamon rolls in the oven, top shelf, 350F. They've been rising overnight and looked pillowy when I slid them in. How long should I bake them?
turn_2_user: Okay it's 8:15 now and I'm back at the oven. Something's happening in there. What do you see?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean describe the rolls as they are in the oven right now, not how they looked before baking.
notes: Temporal state change. Same scene after time passes. T2 already relies on "what do you see" — no v2 optimization needed. World knowledge about cinnamon-roll timing cannot substitute for actually looking.

turn_1_user: I'm at my desk staring at my monitor. I've got a bug ticket open in Jira. It's a P1 about the checkout API returning 500s on cart-update. Suggested fix says 'check the redis lock'. Worth pinging the SRE on-call?
turn_2_user: Switched windows. Is this a production incident, and should I page someone?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the window I just switched to, not the Jira ticket.
notes: Screen-domain swap. T2 already minimal — no v2 optimization needed. The wearable is looking at a screen, not a physical scene. Failure pattern: model answers "yes, page" on the strength of the Jira P1 rather than pivoting.

turn_1_user: I'm in the backyard. There's a buck in the corner, white-tailed, maybe six points, just standing by the maple tree. Should I move slowly to avoid spooking him?
turn_2_user: Okay, which direction did he bolt?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on what you saw before he ran.
notes: Absence detection with directional recall. Visual context shifts because the object left, not because the user moved. T2 already uses pure pronoun "he" — no v2 optimization needed. World knowledge about deer behavior is not sufficient.

turn_1_user: I'm at the warehouse pulling holiday stock. There are 12 cases of the cabernet on this pallet. Typical ramp says we sell about 8 cases per week through the holiday window. Should I pull more from the back?
turn_2_user: Walked over to another pallet. Same question — should I pull more from the back?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the pallet I'm standing at now, not the cabernet.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names "chardonnay" (product identity leak removed). Numeric/quantitative swap. Failure pattern: model carries over the 12-case math. The image-enabled slice places a chardonnay pallet with a different case count in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm in the baby aisle. I just picked up the formula we've been trying. The label says it's 'Stage 2 infant formula, for 6 to 12 months, made with organic cow's milk, no corn syrup solids, 20 calories per fluid ounce'. Is this the right stage for my 7-month-old?
turn_2_user: I just put it back on the shelf. What did the label say the calorie count was?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on what you just saw on the label before I put it back.
notes: Object-label prior recall. Kept verbatim in v2 because T2 already relies on prior recall via "the label" — no stage-direction leak. Failure pattern: model refuses or clarifies instead of reaching back.

turn_1_user: I'm in the nursery. Just mounted a white floating shelf above the changing table for diapers and wipes. Does the level look right?
turn_2_user: Moved to a different room. Any suggestion for this wall?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the wall I'm looking at in the new room, not the nursery shelf.
notes: Spatial shift from nursery to family room. T2 does not name the destination; deictic "this wall" must resolve to the current frame. Extends ex-09 current-frame shape with a decor question.

turn_1_user: I'm in the paint aisle of the hardware store. I've got a gallon of eggshell latex in a warm white called 'Cotton Balls'. Planning to do our hallway. Good call on sheen?
turn_2_user: Over in a different aisle now. What about this?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean whatever I'm standing in front of now, not the paint.
notes: Spatial shift across retail aisles. T2 deictic "this" carries all the weight. Image-enabled slice places the wearable in the flooring aisle. Multi-referent because flooring aisles present many products at once.

turn_1_user: At my workbench. The table saw has a ripping blade on it — 24-tooth carbide, good for cross-cuts of 2x4s. I'm about to cut studs to 92 and a quarter inches. Blade height okay?
turn_2_user: Switched stations. Is this one set up right?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the tool I'm standing at now, not the table saw.
notes: Spatial shift within a workshop to a different power tool. Distractor_present because multiple tools are within view at any station. Failure pattern: model carries blade-height guidance to a completely different tool class.

turn_1_user: I'm holding a ripe Hass avocado. Skin is dark, gives slightly when I squeeze. Safe for guacamole tonight?
turn_2_user: Picked up a different one. How's this one look?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the avocado I'm holding now, not the first one.
notes: Object swap in the same location. T2 relies on "this one" — no verbal leak about the new avocado's state. Failure pattern: model carries first avocado's assessment.

turn_1_user: At my desk. Holding a refurbished ThinkPad T14, 16 gigs of RAM, bought for my kid's middle school. Battery test looked solid. Reasonable first laptop?
turn_2_user: Picked up something else. How does this compare?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the device in my hand now, not the ThinkPad.
notes: Object swap on a desk — second device is not named in T2. Failure pattern: model makes up specs or carries over ThinkPad assessment.

turn_1_user: At the rack. Holding a 45-pound barbell with clips on. Doing a deadlift warm-up, three sets of five at 95. Bar spin feel off?
turn_2_user: Swapped for something else on the rack. Is this the right one for my next set?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the bar I picked up just now, not the 45-pound one.
notes: Gym equipment swap. Multi-referent because a rack contains several bar types. Correct answer depends on which bar is actually in hand.

turn_1_user: Poured hot sugar syrup into the candy molds five minutes ago. Still glassy and translucent — I can see liquid settle when I tap the tray. When can I pop them out?
turn_2_user: Checking back now. Can I demold these?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on how they look right now, not when I poured.
notes: Temporal state evolution of the same scene. Answer requires visual check of opacity/set. Failure pattern: model retells T1 liquid-state description.

turn_1_user: Just started the grill. Coals are red-hot. Set the ribeyes on the grates at 7:02. Two minutes a side.
turn_2_user: It's 7:08 now. How are they?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Describe what the steaks look like on the grates right now.
notes: Temporal state check on grill. Answer is visual (sear/color).

turn_1_user: At a consignment shop. Just pulled a denim jacket off the rack — vintage Levi's, size medium, small patch over the left breast pocket. Fair for thirty-eight dollars?
turn_2_user: Put it back. I'm holding a different one now. This one worth it?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the jacket I'm holding now, not the Levi's.
notes: Original referent returned to rack, new item in hand — current-frame question. Distinct from sc-05 (swap in hand with no return) and from sc-11 (return + prior recall).

turn_1_user: Drafting a Slack message to a client thanking them for renewing. Leaning toward a warm tone. Should I keep it casual or add a formal sign-off?
turn_2_user: Actually, what about this one instead?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the draft I have open right now, not the thank-you message.
notes: Pure verbal-deictic: no location change, same screen, different draft. Failure pattern: model answers about the thank-you message. Theoretical shape — no pilot anchor.

turn_1_user: Holding a pair of slip-joint pliers, plastic-dipped handles, seven-inch. Right tool for pulling a bent finishing nail out of trim?
turn_2_user: What about this one?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the tool I have in my hand now, not the pliers.
notes: Pure verbal-deictic tool swap, no location movement. Failure pattern: model assumes the question is still about pliers.

turn_1_user: In the driver's seat. Dashboard shows the check-engine light, fuel at a quarter, trip reads 412 miles. Can I safely make it another 40 miles to the next rest stop?
turn_2_user: Pulled into a gas station. Something else is flashing now. What's this telling me?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the icon flashing on the dash right now, not the check-engine light from before.
notes: Blended cue: spatial (pulled over), temporal (minutes passed), and an additional dashboard symbol. Distractor_present because check-engine from T1 pulls attention. Failure pattern: model conflates the two dash warnings.

turn_1_user: At the reference desk. Just finished cataloging a box of donated atlases into the oversize section — six of them on the second shelf from the top, spine-out, no Dewey assigned yet.
turn_2_user: Back at my computer. Which shelf did I put those on?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on where I shelved the atlases a few minutes ago.
notes: Prior recall across a short spatial shift. T1 fully specifies the answer; easy tier. Extends ex-08.

turn_1_user: In the guest bedroom prepping for my in-laws. I put the spare reading glasses on the nightstand next to the water carafe and tucked the phone charger behind the lamp.
turn_2_user: Downstairs now. Where did I stash the charger?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on where I put it in the guest bedroom.
notes: Prior recall of one item among several. Multi_referent (glasses, carafe, charger) tests whether the model retrieves the correct one.

turn_1_user: Holding the spark plug I just pulled from the mower. Electrode is charred black, porcelain is darkened. Replacing it with the new NGK from the package.
turn_2_user: New plug is in. What color was the old one again?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on the plug I showed you before I swapped it.
notes: Object-swap with prior recall of the departed object's state. T1 provides the answer in text.

turn_1_user: At a boutique fitting room. Holding up a silk blouse — ivory, button-front, French cuffs, size small. Planning to wear it to a rehearsal dinner.
turn_2_user: Trying a different one on now. Was the first one's color closer to bone or true white?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on the blouse I held up at the start.
notes: Prior recall of a color adjective stated in T1. Tests whether the model reaches back to the first referent rather than answering about the new one.

turn_1_user: Pulled a tray of short ribs from the sous vide at 3pm — 48-hour cook at 135. Beautiful pink slices, dripping jus.
turn_2_user: It's 6 now. How did the ribs look when I pulled them?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on how they looked at 3pm.
notes: Prior recall across a multi-hour gap. Stresses the time_gap=hours bucket — most v1 scenarios were seconds or minutes.

turn_1_user: Parked at a Starbucks drive-thru. There's a silver Subaru two cars ahead of me. Driver is in a red flannel, passenger is looking at a phone.
turn_2_user: They just drove off. Did you see which way they turned out?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on the Subaru that was ahead of me a minute ago.
notes: Object departure with directional recall. Turn direction is not stated in T1 — the model must have actually tracked the visual. Mirrors sc-09 (deer) in a different domain.

turn_1_user: At the block party. My neighbor Carla walked up with a casserole. She's wearing a floral apron, dark jeans, hair in a clip. I helped her carry a second dish from her car.
turn_2_user: She's gone. Which direction did she leave?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on which way she walked off.
notes: Object departure in a social setting. Direction is not verbal — visual tracking required.

turn_1_user: Picking in bay 14. Just grabbed a case of part number MXR-4407. Two stickers on the box: a blue 'heavy' label and an orange 'this side up' decal. Expiration printed as 2028-03.
turn_2_user: Case is back on the rack. What was the expiration date on the label?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on the label I just showed you.
notes: Object-return with prior recall — distinct from sc-11 (baby aisle) by domain and by the presence of multiple label elements (heavy sticker, orientation sticker, expiration).

turn_1_user: On my monitor: three charts, Q3 revenue by region. EMEA at 4.2 million, NA at 5.1 million, APAC at 2.8 million. Drafting the board deck.
turn_2_user: Different doc open now, another hour later. Which region was highest?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on the Q3 chart I was showing you earlier.
notes: Blended prior: object_shift (new doc on screen) plus temporal_state (an hour later). Multi_referent because three regions compete. Tests prior-recall with arithmetic comparison.

turn_1_user: In the back corner of my garage. I've got a box marked 'camping'. Inside: two sleeping bags, a lantern, a four-burner stove. Setting up for next weekend.
turn_2_user: Different area now. Can you tell me what I should bring?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I didn't tell you where I'm going, what trip, or how long — ask me.
notes: Clarify class: T2 is genuinely under-anchored. Correct behavior is to ask which trip, what duration, or what conditions. Failure pattern: model invents a plausible camping list.

turn_1_user: Holding a chef's knife, eight-inch, carbon steel. I'm dicing shallots for mignonette. Grip look good?
turn_2_user: Holding something different now. Is the technique the same?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I didn't tell you what I'm holding — ask.
notes: Clarify class: "something different" is a deictic with no visible anchor in text. Correct behavior: ask what tool. Failure pattern: model assumes another knife.

turn_1_user: On the couch. The TV is on ESPN, my laptop has fantasy football rankings open, and I've got my phone in my hand. Busy evening.
turn_2_user: What do you think about this?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I haven't said which one I mean — TV, laptop, or phone. Ask me.
notes: Clarify class with multi_referent: three candidate referents. "This" alone is insufficient. Correct behavior: ask which of the three.

turn_1_user: On the front porch. Two squirrels are chasing each other around the maple. One has a longer tail and lighter fur on the belly.
turn_2_user: Something just ran off. What did it look like?
turn_3_repair_anchor: You didn't say which one — ask which I meant.
notes: Clarify with object_departure: two plausible referents departed. Model should ask which. Failure pattern: model describes a generic squirrel.

turn_1_user: At the rehearsal dinner. Two cousins just arrived — Priya and Anita. Priya is in a red sari, Anita is in a green kurta. They're getting drinks at the bar.
turn_2_user: What did she say to you?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I didn't tell you anyone spoke to me, and I didn't say which cousin — ask.
notes: Clarify class, blended cue: multi_referent (two cousins) plus implied temporal gap. Model must both ask who and note no speech event was described.

turn_1_user: Just woke up. Bedroom is dark, curtains drawn. Nothing else on.
turn_2_user: What's the weather like outside right now?
turn_3_repair_anchor: You can't see through walls — say you don't have a frame that would show weather.
notes: Abstain class: no reachable visual frame can answer. Correct behavior: decline and suggest opening the curtain. Failure pattern: model guesses or fetches a weather API answer.

turn_1_user: At my desk. Have Outlook open — I can see today's meetings: 10am standup, 1pm 1:1, 3pm exec review.
turn_2_user: What's on my calendar tomorrow?
turn_3_repair_anchor: The tomorrow view isn't open — tell me you can't see it from the current screen.
notes: Abstain class with a future-state time gap. Today's view is visible; tomorrow's is not. Correct behavior: decline, optionally suggest switching views.

turn_1_user: At the circulation desk. A patron dropped off a return — a hardcover novel, dust-jacketed. I slid it into the cart without looking at the cover.
turn_2_user: What was the book's title?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I told you I didn't look at the cover — say you don't have that information.
notes: Abstain class: object has returned to an unobservable location and the relevant detail was never observed. Correct: decline. Failure pattern: model invents a plausible title.

turn_1_user: Driving northbound on I-5. Passenger window is down. Quiet traffic.
turn_2_user: Tell me what that sign said.
turn_3_repair_anchor: I haven't pointed out any sign — say you don't have one to describe.
notes: Abstain class, verbal_deictic with no established referent. "That sign" has no anchor anywhere in the session. Correct: decline. Failure pattern: model fabricates a sign.
